problem_statement: Checked-baggage pricing uses the wrong haul classification: flights under 6 hours are being treated as long-haul and charged €70, while flights 6 hours or longer are being treated as short-haul and charged €35.
goal: Fix the baggage fee classification so that short-haul flights (< 6 hours) receive the short-haul checked-baggage rate (€35) and long-haul flights (>= 6 hours) receive the long-haul rate (€70).
acceptance_criteria: A checked bag added to a flight shorter than 6 hours is priced at 35.00 when no explicit price is provided.
A checked bag added to a flight of 6 hours or longer is priced at 70.00 when no explicit price is provided.
Existing explicit baggage prices, if provided, continue to override the default haul-based rate logic.
constraints: Likely limited to backend pricing logic in booking service; no frontend changes expected.
The fix should preserve the existing haul-threshold behavior at 6 hours, only correcting the inversion.
unknowns: Whether the haul helper is used anywhere else beyond checked-baggage pricing.
Whether tests already exist for haul classification and need only inversion updates or additional coverage.
repo_summary: Backend service-driven application; booking and flight logic live under backend/app/services, with tests under backend/tests and backend/app/tests. The issue explicitly points to backend/app/services/booking_service.py and the helper _is_long_haul.

targeted_symbols.reason: Explicitly identified in the issue as the affected logic; likely contains the inverted 6-hour threshold used to choose baggage pricing.

localization_rationale: The issue description directly names the affected file and helper: backend/app/services/booking_service.py (_is_long_haul).
The symptom is a pure classification inversion at the 6-hour boundary, which suggests a small helper or conditional rather than a broad pricing subsystem change.
Booking tests are the most likely place to validate the corrected behavior and prevent regression.
